identity.name: Theodore V. Lango
identity.title: Workforce Transformation Strategist
identity.tagline: Building Adaptive Systems for the Human-AI Era
identity.scaffolding_philosophy: I don't build AI solutions — I build the scaffolding that makes AI solutions work. Systems where deterministic code handles precision and probabilistic models handle uncertainty. AI that actually works in enterprise operations.

core_thesis: Organizations don't have to choose between cost efficiency, customer experience, and employee satisfaction — they can optimize for all three simultaneously through adaptive systems.
the_moment:
We're entering an era where the distribution of work between human agents and AI agents will be fluid — ebbing and flowing based on technology maturity, regulatory shifts, cost dynamics, and customer expectations.

**Organizations that build rigid systems optimized for today's distribution will be disrupted.**
**Organizations that build adaptive systems designed for continuous rebalancing will thrive.**

The organizations winning with AI aren't using smarter models — they're building better scaffolding. I build the infrastructure that connects human expertise to AI capability.

The future won't be all-AI or all-human — it will settle into a dynamic equilibrium across distinct pools of work, each requiring fundamentally different planning models. Traditional Erlang and Poisson-based staffing assumes homogeneous queues and interchangeable agents. That assumption collapses when AI handles routine work autonomously, humans collaborate with AI on moderate complexity, and specialists own the work that demands judgment and empathy. The math itself demands a new approach — one built on value-based routing, cognitive portfolio theory, and second-order effect quantification.

telos.mission: Transform how organizations deploy, manage, and optimize knowledge workers at scale — from reactive scheduling to adaptive, intelligent systems that respect both operational needs and human potential.

problems.title: Deterministic thinking in a stochastic world
problems.description: Most WFM teams use point estimates when probability distributions would serve them better.

problems.title: Workforce as cost center, not strategic lever
problems.description: Organizations optimize for efficiency alone, missing the multi-objective opportunity.

problems.title: Unpreparedness for human-AI workforce evolution
problems.description: Rigid systems optimized for today will be disrupted. Adaptive systems will thrive.

problems.title: Misapplying AI where code suffices, misapplying code where AI excels
problems.description: Organizations waste resources building AI for deterministic problems while missing probabilistic opportunities.

problems.title: Vendor promises ignore second-order effects
problems.description: AI containment projections assume linear savings, but demand rebound, complexity concentration, and escalation cascades erode 50-75% of projected value. Organizations need planning models that account for what happens after deflection, not just the deflection itself.

goals.title: Transformation Leadership
goals.description: Lead workforce transformation at scale in the right organization

goals.title: Thought Leadership
goals.description: Grow WFM Labs as the home for advancing the craft

goals.title: Systems Building
goals.description: Build AI-augmented infrastructure as a force multiplier

models.name: StoicGraph
models.description: Relationship progression through trust zones: Aware → Interested → Trust → Advocate. Warm paths over cold outreach.
models.application: Job search targeting, network development, relationship building

models.name: WFM Maturity Model
models.description: 5-level framework for assessing and advancing workforce management capability. From reactive (Level 1) to pioneering (Level 5).

models.name: Three-Engine Architecture
models.description: Network (opportunities) → Content (visibility) → Commerce (sustainability). Each engine reinforces the others.

models.name: The Algorithm
models.description: Current State → Ideal State via verifiable iteration. Define success criteria before executing. Always capture learnings.

models.name: FOW-Value Model
models.description: Three-pool workforce planning framework replacing single-queue Erlang with value-based routing across Autonomous AI, Collaborative Human+AI, and Specialist pools. Each pool uses pool-specific staffing mathematics. Quantifies second-order effects — demand rebound, complexity concentration, escalation cascades — that erode 50-75% of projected AI savings.
models.evidence_base: 207 research sources, 600+ evidence claims, 94 calibrated parameters
models.application: Workforce planning for AI-augmented operations, realistic savings projections, transformation roadmaps

models.name: Service Demand Rebound Model (SDRM)
models.description: Adapted from transportation economics (Duranton & Turner's Fundamental Law of Road Congestion) to prove that AI deflection creates induced demand through three channels: direct rebound (same issue contacted more easily), indirect emergence (new inquiry types from freed customer bandwidth), and systemic growth (better service drives higher retention and larger base). Short-run elasticity 0.3-0.6, long-run 0.6-1.0.
models.key_insight: 50% AI containment delivers only 25-35% actual FTE savings in Year 1, declining to 15-25% by Year 3 without active management
models.application: AI ROI validation, vendor promise reality-checking, transformation business cases

beliefs: WFM is applied operations research, not spreadsheet management
The goal isn't prediction accuracy — it's decision quality under uncertainty
Monte Carlo beats point estimates, every time
Book before resume — demonstrate thinking, not just credentials
Triadic closure is the mechanism for warm introductions
Scaffolding > Model — system architecture matters more than which AI you use
AI amplifies, doesn't replace — human judgment remains essential
Automate the repeatable, focus on the novel

methodologies.description: Business situation diagnosis — Startup, Turnaround, Realignment, Sustaining success

methodologies.description: Goals, Roles, Processes, Interpersonal relationships, Technology alignment

methodologies.name: Service Profit Chain
methodologies.description: Employee satisfaction → Customer satisfaction → Profitability linkage

executive_education: Ongoing professional development in AI/ML applications

what_i_offer.title: Multi-Objective Optimization Expertise
what_i_offer.description: Most organizations treat workforce planning as a zero-sum game: cut costs OR improve service, invest in employees OR hit budget. Through adaptive capacity planning, real-time automation, and systems designed for dynamic trade-offs along a Pareto frontier, I help organizations satisfy competing stakeholder needs — customers, employees, and shareholders — simultaneously.

what_i_offer.title: Transformation Leadership at Scale
what_i_offer.description: Led workforce transformation across 85+ contact centers handling 400M+ interactions annually. The pattern matters more than the scale: MetLife earning industry-best WFM recognition, Comcast capturing $50M+ in efficiency gains, Intradiem evolving its product strategy — a repeatable methodology for advancing operational maturity while delivering measurable results.

what_i_offer.title: Technology + Operations Bridge
what_i_offer.description: I operate at the intersection of strategy, operations, and technology. Led platform selections, designed automation architectures, and built analytical models progressing from early-generation BI tools through modern Python-based analytics and AI workflows. I understand both what technology can do and what operations actually need — accelerating transformation timelines and reducing implementation risk.

what_i_offer.title: AI Infrastructure Design
what_i_offer.description: Three years deep on practical AI application — not prompt engineering theater, but building chained workflows combining deterministic and probabilistic methods, leading internal AI education initiatives, and co-inventing patented approaches to intelligent workforce optimization. I understand where AI creates genuine leverage and where traditional methods remain superior.

what_i_offer.title: Published Framework & Thought Leadership
what_i_offer.description: Author of 'Adaptive: Building Workforce Systems for an (Unpredictable) Future' — a thesis backed by 20 years of proof points. The book provides a maturity model, implementation frameworks, and strategic perspective for the human-AI collaboration era. Founder of WFM Labs community with 1,000+ members.

what_i_offer.title: Value-Based Planning for the Three-Pool Workforce
what_i_offer.description: Developed the FOW-Value Model — a research-backed framework proving that AI workforce savings erode 50-75% through second-order effects (demand rebound, complexity concentration, escalation cascades) that traditional planning ignores. The model replaces single-queue Erlang with value-based routing across three pools: Autonomous AI, Collaborative Human+AI, and Specialist — each with pool-specific staffing mathematics. Grounded in 207 research sources with 94 calibrated parameters. Live at valuemodel.wfmlabs.com.

seeking.strategy: Relationship-first approach (StoicGraph methodology). Book as calling card, not resume. Warm introductions over cold outreach.

clarity_pipeline.description: Organizations don't just need AI — they need scaffolding appropriate to their maturity.

tiers.name: Legacy Enterprises
tiers.challenge: Change aversion, established processes, regulatory complexity
tiers.path: AI as efficiency tool → strategic capability
tiers.scaffolding: Integration with existing systems, gradual capability building, change management focus

tiers.name: Modern Enterprises
tiers.challenge: Digital transformation mid-flight, legacy + modern hybrid
tiers.scaffolding: Hybrid human-AI workflows, retraining programs, platform modernization

tiers.name: Digital Natives
tiers.challenge: Building AI-first from ground up, avoiding over-automation
tiers.scaffolding: Avoiding over-automation, maintaining human judgment, building sustainable practices

clarity_pipeline.conclusion: The path from reactive scheduling to AI-augmented operations varies by organizational DNA. I build context-specific frameworks that meet organizations where they are.

narratives.origin_story: I spent 20+ years in contact center operations and workforce management. I've been the WFM analyst staring at spreadsheets at 2am. I've been the operations director trying to hit SLAs with half the staff I needed. I've been the consultant telling executives what they didn't want to hear. Along the way, I kept asking: why are we doing this the hard way? Why are we using deterministic math in a stochastic world? So I started building better approaches. Operations research methods. Probabilistic planning. Multi-objective optimization. Eventually, I wrote a book about it — Adaptive. Not to become famous, but because I got tired of explaining the same concepts in every engagement.
narratives.transformation_agent: I'm not interested in maintaining what exists. I'm interested in building what should exist. Every role I've taken, I've transformed something. Built new tools. Changed how teams thought about the problem. Left things better than I found them. That's what I'm looking for next — not a job, but a transformation opportunity.
narratives.builder: I build things. Tools, teams, systems, content. The ROC at MetLife. The WFM Labs Maturity Model. TARS, my AI assistant infrastructure. The Contact Center Compass newsletter. I see a problem, I want to build a solution. Not talk about solutions. Build them.
narratives.thought_leader: I didn't set out to be a thought leader. I just wanted to solve problems better, and I started writing about what I learned. 1,000+ newsletter subscribers. A published book. A community of practitioners who care about advancing the craft. It happened because I kept sharing what I was learning, and it turned out other people wanted to learn it too.
